title: Dracula
author: Stoker, Bram
summary: Jonathan Harker, a young English solicitor, travels from Munich and Vienna into the Carpathians to visit Count Dracula’s castle. He records impressions of Transylvania’s peoples, food, and superstitions—paprika dishes, mamaliga, and fearful villagers who cross themselves and warn him on the eve of St. George. At the Golden Krone he receives a courteous letter from Dracula; an anxious landlady gives him a rosary and crucifix. On the coach toward the Borgo Pass passengers name demons—Ordog, pokol, stregoica, vrolok—and hint at vampiric dangers. Harker’s journal mixes practical notes with growing unease as he nears the castle, and fears for his safety.

characters.name: Jonathan Harker
characters.personality: observant, dutiful, pragmatic; keeps a detailed journal
characters.lifespan: Arrives at Castle Dracula and records events in his journal; fate beyond this excerpt is not revealed here

characters.name: Count Dracula
characters.personality: polite, mysterious, foreboding; nobleman of Transylvania
characters.lifespan: Addressed in letters and awaited by Harker; presence established but ultimate fate not shown in excerpt

characters.name: Mina Murray (Mina Harker)
characters.personality: beloved, practical (referred to as recipient of Harker's notes)
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as the person to whom Harker hopes his papers will go; no fate given in excerpt

characters.lifespan: Mentioned in contents only; fate not given in excerpt
